completed_work: Added max_warning_admissions to latest-context manifest assembly.
Verified with full pytest suite: 179 passed.
key_decisions: Keep max_warning_admissions optional and default it to None so current behavior remains unchanged.
Represent capped warning-admitted items as policy exclusions and count them in admission_summary.excluded_by_policy.
next_actions: Decide whether provider latest-context commands should use a default warning cap for admission-gated runs, or keep requiring explicit --max-warning-admissions.
risks: The cap is opt-in; callers must choose a cap value before warning-admitted context is limited.
summary_text: AI-Lab implemented the next C ergonomics slice: an optional admit_with_warning cap for latest-context assembly. context_pack_builder.py now has cap_admit_with_warning_items and build_latest_context_manifest accepts max_warning_admissions. By default the value is None, so existing behavior is unchanged. When a cap is set, only the first N admit_with_warning items are retained and later warning-admitted items are policy-excluded with a note explaining that the cap was exceeded. The manifest admission_summary reflects capped warning exclusions through excluded_by_policy. prompt_context.py passes the option through, and build_context_pack.py, ask_provider.py, and compare_providers.py expose --max-warning-admissions. Tests were updated for helper behavior, manifest integration, prompt-context passthrough, and CLI/provider wiring. The full suite passed with 179 tests.
